Fixed removing hard limits expanding lower y-range labels actually render. R/render.R. Terminal states extended fake stays (example data showed ~4.75 h “Discharged” box invented median tail fallback). New opt-terminal_activities parameter: terminal final move gets zero duration renders vertical marker italic direct label, outside fill legend. R/plot_patient_journey.R, R/transform.R, R/render.R. zero-width-nudge message lied (“Stored duration unaffected” duration computed nudge). duration now computed nudging (-timestamp stay stores 0) message says . R/transform.R. nudged box hidden behind successor (-timestamp moves). New render-xmin_render stagger; true xmin untouched event assignment unaffected. Known limitation: 3+ moves sharing one timestamp still compress sliver widths — acceptable, documented. R/transform.R, R/render.R. Three cryptic crash paths now abort cleanly actionable messages: typo’d tail_strategy (match.arg, R/utils.R); exclude_categories removing every location event (post-exclusion re-check, R/plot_patient_journey.R, plus defensive n_boxes==0 guard R/transform.R); vector/NA case_id (scalar guard, R/validate.R). R/plot_patient_journey.R, R/validate.R. patient_col mandatory. patient_col = NULL now supported logs secondary identifier; title falls back "Case <id>". R/validate.R, R/plot_patient_journey.R. Legend clipping/truncation (two side--side legends overflowed plot width). Legends now stack vertically. R/render.R. Files: new R/schema.R, new tests/testthat/test-schema.R, edit R/plot_patient_journey.R. event_log_schema() constructor: revision 1 — time_col/act_type_col/activity_col/case_col/patient_col/location_categories, returns classed list, print() method. autodetect_schema(data, location_categories = NULL) — CORRECTED SPEC: Candidate lists (case-insensitive): - time_col: timestamp, time, ts, datetime, date_time, event_time, event_time_stamp - case_col: case_id, caseid, case, spell_id, episode_id, encounter_id, complaint_id, ticket_id — id removed: matches surrogate/row key likely wrong right. - act_type_col: act_type, event_type, category, type, event_category - activity_col: activity, label, description, event_name, name — bare event removed (fuzzy distance ≤ 2 event_type made one column claimable two roles). - patient_col: patient_id, patient, k_number, mrn, person_id Resolution algorithm (replaces revision 1’s per-role independent matching): 1. Roles resolved fixed order time → case → act_type → activity → patient. data column may claimed one role; claimed removed later roles’ consideration. 2. Per role: exact case-insensitive match first; else adist() ≤ 2 fuzzy match (reuse suggest_matches()). 3. two unclaimed columns tie one role (equal distance), abort naming asking explicit event_log_schema() — never pick silently. 4. Required roles (patient) unresolved → abort naming , columns tried, event_log_schema() escape hatch. 5. success, cli_inform one line per field, stating exact vs fuzzy. Wiring plot_patient_journey(): schema = NULL param. Precedence per field, highest wins: explicit individual argument → schema field → current hardcoded default. Autodetect explicit sentinel schema = "auto" — never silently. Tests: construction/print; fuzzy recovery renamed example_journey; tie → abort; nonsense columns → abort naming roles; per-field precedence (explicit act_type_col beats schema, time_col comes schema); claimed-column exclusivity (column matching time activity lists consumed time ); earlier suites pass untouched schema argument. A tibble: 11 × 5 #>    case_id timestamp           act_type     activity category #>    <chr>   <dttm>              <chr>        <chr>    <chr>    #>  1 A       2024-01-01 08:00:00 state_change Received Hardware #>  2 A       2024-01-01 08:10:00 triage_time  Triage   Hardware #>  3 A       2024-01-01 11:30:00 state_change Review   Hardware #>  4 A       2024-01-01 18:00:00 state_change Closed   Hardware #>  5 B       2024-01-01 09:15:00 state_change Received Software #>  6 B       2024-01-01 09:20:00 triage_time  Triage   Software #>  7 B       2024-01-01 12:00:00 state_change Closed   Software #>  8 C       2024-01-01 10:00:00 state_change Received Access   #>  9 C       2024-01-01 10:05:00 triage_time  Triage   Access   #> 10 C       2024-01-01 13:00:00 state_change Review   Access   #> 11 C       2024-01-02 09:00:00 state_change Closed   Access plot_case_timeline(   long, case_id = "A",   case_col = "case_id", state_events = "state_change" )

title: Cohort analysis
text: plot_cohort_timeline() lays several cases one facet panel per case. default (case_ids = NULL) plots every case data, max_cases guard (25, since faceting hundreds cases hang, plot — pass explicit case_ids raise ):  panel gets time axis default (align_start = FALSE), absolute dates line across panels — useful spotting whether several complaints affected event (staffing gap, system outage). state keeps fill colour every panel: palette built , union states across whole cohort, independently per panel.
code: plot_cohort_timeline(   complaint_example,   state_events = "stage_change", case_col = "complaint_id",   terminal_activities = "Formal letter sent",   state_label = "Stage" )

title: Cohort analysis
text: compare durations rather dates, rebase every case elapsed hours first state event align_start = TRUE. panels share one +Nh axis:  Now ’s immediately visible "CMP-03"’s bar runs far longer others — stall “review” vignette keeps coming back .
code: plot_cohort_timeline(   complaint_example,   state_events = "stage_change", case_col = "complaint_id",   terminal_activities = "Formal letter sent",   state_label = "Stage", align_start = TRUE )

title: Cohort analysis
text: Faceting good eyeballing handful cases; whole cohort, eventviz also numeric summarisers built box-derivation pipeline, describe exactly plots draw.

title: Cohort analysis
text: Every duration statistic package respects end_inferred: case’s final state recorded exit, dwell imputed rendering convenience, data. default ’s excluded mean/median/ quantiles (n_inferred_excluded reports many dropped); pass include_inferred = TRUE fold back .
code: summarise_state_durations(   complaint_example,   state_events = "stage_change", case_col = "complaint_id" ) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   state      n_cases mean_secs median_secs p25_secs p75_secs n_inferred_excluded #>   <chr>        <int>     <dbl>       <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>               <int> #> 1 Acknowled…       8   108000        86400    86400   140400                   0 #> 2 Assigned         8    97200        86400    86400    97200                   0 #> 3 Formal le…       7       NA           NA       NA       NA                   7 #> 4 Senior re…       7   172800       172800   129600   216000                   0 #> 5 Triage           8    91800        86400    86400    86400                   0 #> 6 Under rev…       8   487543.      259200   216000   388800                   1

title: Cohort analysis
text: summarise_breach_rate() reports fraction cases exceed target, either across whole case (scope = "case") within one named state (scope = "<state name>", e.g. SLA long complaint may sit “review”):
code: breach <- summarise_breach_rate(   complaint_example, target_hours = 24 * 7, scope = "Under review",   state_events = "stage_change", case_col = "complaint_id" ) breach #> # A tibble: 7 × 4 #>   case_id elapsed_hours breached end_inferred #>   <chr>           <dbl> <lgl>    <lgl>        #> 1 CMP-01             72 FALSE    FALSE        #> 2 CMP-02             36 FALSE    FALSE        #> 3 CMP-03            504 TRUE     FALSE        #> 4 CMP-05             72 FALSE    FALSE        #> 5 CMP-06             48 FALSE    FALSE        #> 6 CMP-07             72 FALSE    FALSE        #> 7 CMP-08            144 FALSE    FALSE attr(breach, "breach_rate") #> [1] 0.1428571

title: Cohort analysis

code: plot_transition_summary(   complaint_example,   state_events = "stage_change", case_col = "complaint_id" )

title: Getting started
text: plot_case_timeline() expects one row per event, (minimum) case identifier, timestamp, event-type column marking rows open new exclusive state, activity/label column: act_type == "ed_location_move" "location_move" marks move new location; everything else (obs, clerk_review, test_ordered, …) instantaneous point event gets plotted timeline doesn’t create new box. tell eventviz values open state via state_events — ’s default, guessing: omit , error lists exactly ’s data.
code: head(example_journey) #>   case_id           timestamp         act_type                      activity #> 1  SP-001 2024-03-15 08:30:00 ed_location_move          Emergency Department #> 2  SP-001 2024-03-15 08:36:00     clerk_review Ambulance handover documented #> 3  SP-001 2024-03-15 08:45:00           triage  Triage complete - Category 2 #> 4  SP-001 2024-03-15 09:15:00              obs    BP 145/90, HR 88, SpO2 97% #> 5  SP-001 2024-03-15 09:30:00     clerk_review    Initial nursing assessment #> 6  SP-001 2024-03-15 09:45:00     test_ordered       FBC, U&E, CRP, Troponin

title: Getting started
text: example_journey one case, case_id didn’t need supplied — eventviz used one available said . coloured box state case occupied interval; point instantaneous event, positioned midline whichever box fell inside. legend splits state fills event-type shapes/colours two never share hue default (colourblind-safe "okabe" palette — see ?journey_palette "brewer" alternative).
code: plot_case_timeline(example_journey,                    state_events = c("location_move", "ed_location_move")) #> ℹ `case_id` not supplied; using the only case "SP-001".

title: Getting started
text: formatted duration appears non-terminal box. box’s end time inferred (data feed stopped recording next state event), label carries trailing "+" plot never overclaims precision doesn’t .
code: plot_case_timeline(example_journey,                    state_events = c("location_move", "ed_location_move"),                    show_duration = TRUE) #> ℹ `case_id` not supplied; using the only case "SP-001".

title: Getting started
text: Without information, final box case natural end — plot_case_timeline() infer one (default, extending last recorded event). case reaches genuinely terminal state — “Discharged”, “Closed”, “Formal letter sent” — inference wrong: case didn’t spend hours “Discharged”. Tell function activities terminal renders instantaneous marker instead:  case’s data feed simply stops reaching terminal state, box’s right edge drawn open (dashed edge italic "(ongoing)" label) rather silently inventing end — see linear processes vignette worked example support_ticket_example’s still-open ticket.
code: plot_case_timeline(   example_journey, case_id = "SP-001",   state_events = c("location_move", "ed_location_move"),   terminal_activities = "Discharged" )

title: Getting started
text: Overlay target/threshold line — useful service standards like 4-hour ED target:  reference_lines takes data frame one row per line: offset_hours (numeric, hours case’s first event) label.
code: plot_case_timeline(   example_journey, case_id = "SP-001",   state_events = c("location_move", "ed_location_move"),   reference_lines = data.frame(offset_hours = 4, label = "4h target") )

title: Getting started
text: presentation-ready plot, label_boxes = TRUE labels box directly centre, event_type_top_n collapses long tail rare event types "" legend stays readable:
code: plot_case_timeline(   example_journey, case_id = "SP-001",   state_events = c("location_move", "ed_location_move"),   label_boxes      = TRUE,   event_type_top_n = 3 ) #> ℹ 2 event type(s) collapsed into "Other" (kept top 3 by frequency): #>   "home_today_change" and "triage".

title: Getting started
text: Pass return_data = TRUE get derived tables alongside plot — useful debugging, building custom visualisation top box/event derivation: summary one row per state stay (case, state, entry/exit, duration, whether end inferred) — shape summarise_case_durations() returns across whole cohort. See cohort analysis vignette aggregating across cases.
code: res <- plot_case_timeline(   example_journey, case_id = "SP-001",   state_events = c("location_move", "ed_location_move"),   return_data = TRUE ) names(res) #> [1] "plot"    "boxes"   "events"  "summary" res$summary #> # A tibble: 4 × 7 #>   case_id state            xmin                xmax                duration_secs #>   <chr>   <chr>            <dttm>              <dttm>                      <dbl> #> 1 SP-001  Emergency Depar… 2024-03-15 08:30:00 2024-03-15 13:15:00         17100 #> 2 SP-001  Acute Medical U… 2024-03-15 13:15:00 2024-03-16 11:30:00         80100 #> 3 SP-001  Discharge Lounge 2024-03-16 11:30:00 2024-03-16 15:00:00         12600 #> 4 SP-001  Discharged       2024-03-16 15:00:00 2024-03-16 19:45:00         17100 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: end_inferred <lgl>, terminal <lgl>

what: Pivot a wide milestone-timestamp event log into long form — pivot_events_longer
title: Pivot a wide milestone-timestamp event log into long form — pivot_events_longer
text: Milestone-style event logs commonly stored wide: one row per case, one column per timestamp (`"arrival_time"`, `"triage_time"`, `"discharge_time"`, ...). [plot_case_timeline()] needs long form (one row per event); reshapes former latter. pivot plot agree box: state columns emit `act_type = "state_change"`, exactly pass `state_events` [plot_case_timeline()].

title: Pivot a wide milestone-timestamp event log into long form — pivot_events_longer
code: pivot_events_longer(   data,   case_col,   time_cols,   state_cols = NULL,   act_type_map = NULL,   activity_map = NULL,   drop_na = TRUE,   tz = "UTC",   time_col_out = "timestamp",   act_type_col_out = "act_type",   activity_col_out = "activity" )

title: Pivot a wide milestone-timestamp event log into long form — pivot_events_longer
text: data Wide data frame, one row per case. case_col Column identifying case. time_cols Character vector wide column names pivot. state_cols Subset `time_cols` mark state change; get `act_type = "state_change"`. act_type_map Named character vector: `time_cols` entry -> act_type, non-state milestones. `NULL` = milestone name used -. activity_map Named character vector: `time_cols` entry -> display label. `NULL` = derived milestone name (see `prettify_milestone()`): strip trailing timestamp suffix, replace separators spaces, title-case. drop_na Logical; drop rows whose milestone timestamp `NA` (milestone happen case). Default `TRUE`. tz Timezone forwarded internal datetime coercion. time_col_out, act_type_col_out, activity_col_out Output column names, matching [plot_case_timeline()]'s `time_col`/`act_type_col`/ `activity_col` arguments.

text: data data frame tibble containing event log. state_events Character vector `act_type` values open state (events create boxes). Required — default. omitted (supplied via `schema`), error lists distinct `act_type` values present `data` row counts, can see pass. case_id single case identifier visualise, `NULL` (default). `case_col` names column exactly one distinct value, `NULL` resolves value automatically; one, `NULL` aborts naming first 10 pointing [plot_cohort_timeline()]. Must stay `NULL` `case_col = NULL`. time_col, act_type_col, activity_col, case_col Column-name mappings. `case_col` may `NULL` event logs case column — single unnamed series (`case_id` must also `NULL`). schema [event_log_schema()] object bundling column-mapping arguments, literal string `"auto"` run [autodetect_schema()], `NULL` (default) ignore. Per-field precedence, highest wins: explicitly supplied individual argument (`time_col`, `case_col`, ...) > matching schema field > function's hardcoded default. tz Timezone used parsing character timestamps (`POSIXct` input keeps `tzone`). terminal_activities Character vector `activity` values terminal states (e.g. `"Discharged"`, `"Closed"`). terminal final state event renders zero-duration marker instead box invented duration. exclude_categories Character vector `act_type` values drop entirely plotting, `NULL`. show_labels Logical; show `ggrepel` labels point events. label_max Maximum label character length truncation. show_duration Logical; show formatted duration label non-terminal box (`end_inferred` boxes get `"+"` suffix). label_boxes Logical; label box directly state name, box centre. reference_lines Data frame `offset_hours` (numeric, hours case's first event) `label`, drawn dashed target-threshold lines, `NULL`. event_type_top_n distinct `act_type` count exceeds , keep top-N frequent event types recode rest `""`. `NULL` = bucketing. lane_col Column `data` whose distinct values become swimlanes point events, drawn state band. `NULL` (default) keeps single midline. lane_height, lane_gap Swimlane geometry, `box_height` units. `NULL` defaults `box_height` `0.05 * box_height` respectively. state_label Fill-legend title. `"State"` default; pass e.g. `"Stage"` `"Status"` linear stage process. state_palette, event_palette Named character vectors (level -> hex colour) overriding automatic palettes, `NULL`. palette_style Auto-palette style used `state_palette`/ `event_palette` `NULL`: `"okabe"` (default, colourblind-safe) `"brewer"` (original Set2/Dark2 palette). box_height Height state band, plot y-units. box_gap_prop Proportion box's width trimmed right edge create thin visual gap adjacent states. title Plot title; `NULL` auto-generates `"Case <case_id>"` unless `case_col = NULL`, case case name title stays `NULL`. tail_strategy Strategy inferring final box's end time: `"last_event"` extends last event, falling back `"median"` `"fixed"`. interactive Logical; render interactive `ggiraph` `girafe` widget instead static ggplot. Requires `ggiraph` package. return_data Logical; `TRUE`, return `list(plot, boxes, events, summary)` instead just plot.

title: eventviz 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Turns original single-file patient-journey timeline script general-purpose event-log visualisation package: location-band timelines, staircase stage diagrams location-less linear processes, cohort facets, aggregate/statistical summaries, interactive renderer, three example datasets spanning healthcare, complaints, support tickets.

title: eventviz 0.1.0
text: Four defects found executed review completed stages, plus packaging-layout corrections: plot_journey_cohort() (absolute-time mode) now sizes cosmetic inter-box gap — box’s minimum render width — facet panel’s time span instead whole cohort’s calendar span. Previously, cohort whose cases months apart rendered every box many times true width. Axis-break selection likewise now sees widest single panel, giving per-panel time labels instead one break sized whole calendar range. cohort-absolute vdiffr baseline re-rendered visually re-approved change. plot_journey_cohort() event_type_top_n: "" bucket now receives colour event palette. Previously cohort palette built raw (unbucketed) event types, "" silently fell back grey na.value. plot_journey_with_summary() now derives bar-chart colours exact fill levels timeline used (including synthetic "(pre-admission)" box) honours palette_style/location_palette passed .... Previously bars rebuilt palette different level set, shifting every hue one position whenever two differed. plot_stage_ladder() stage_targets now bands every visit stage case re-enters, draws firebrick breach excess per visit. Previously first visit banded checked. plot_stage_ladder() stage_targets now draws breach excess open (end-inferred) stage , whenever dwell observed last recorded event already exceeds target — elapsed time lower bound, visible breach proven. excess capped last observed instant, median/fixed-imputed end never inflates . Previously open stage never showed breach . plot_journey_cohort() now draws (ongoing) open-spell marker panel every case never reaches terminal_activities state (previously deferred), gains tail_strategy argument forwarded per case, parity plot_patient_journey(). Packaging: three example datasets moved data/ lazy-loaded .rda files built data-raw/ scripts (documented R/data.R), implementation plan’s Stage 0 specified; LICENSE now uses CRAN’s two-line form full text LICENSE.md; .Rbuildignore patterns anchored; DESCRIPTION declares ggplot2 (>= 3.4.0) (needed scale_linewidth_continuous()) R (>= 3.5).

title: eventviz 0.1.0
text: Added pkgdown site (_pkgdown.yml + pkgdown.yaml GitHub Actions workflow deploying GitHub Pages) four vignettes: getting-started (clinical walkthrough), adapting--data (schema autodetection + wide--long pivot wrapper together, bringing data), linear-processes (band vs. staircase complaints/tickets, stage_targets), cohort-analysis (facets + aggregate/breach/transition summaries). Rewrote README.Rmd lead general framing (“visualise timestamped event log”), quick-start example_journey, “just healthcare” section showing complaint_example (staircase) support_ticket_example (band + cohort).

title: eventviz 0.1.0
text: plot_patient_journey() gains interactive = FALSE. TRUE, returns ggiraph::girafe() widget instead static ggplot: location boxes, terminal-state markers, event points gain hover tooltips. Box tooltips show location, format_duration()-formatted duration, entry/exit times, explicit "(end inferred)" / "(inferred)" caveat box’s end imputed — tooltip must never overclaim end show_duration label may. Requires ggiraph package (Suggests ); requireNamespace() guard aborts install hint ’s missing. Internal: journey_layers() gains opts$interactive switch emits ggiraph’s tooltip-bearing geoms (geom_rect_interactive(), geom_segment_interactive(), geom_text_interactive(), geom_point_interactive()) place static equivalents exactly box/terminal-marker/point-event layers; every layer (duration labels, reference lines, ggrepel event labels) unaffected. New render_journey_plot_interactive() (R/render_interactive.R) calls render_journey_plot() opts$interactive = TRUE wraps result girafe() — layer-building logic duplicated. interactive = FALSE (default) untouched: static layers stay exact pre-Stage-7 ggplot2::geom_* classes, every existing vdiffr baseline still applies unchanged.

title: eventviz 0.1.0
text: New summarise_journey_durations() returns one row per location stay across cohort (case, location, entry/exit, duration, end_inferred, terminal), summarise_stage_durations() builds per-location statistics (case count, mean/median/p25/p75 dwell, n_inferred_excluded). New summarise_breach_rate(data, target_hours, scope = ...) reports fraction cases exceed target. scope = "spell" measures whole-spell elapsed time (first move last event); scope = "<location>" measures dwell within one stage (e.g. ED 4-hour standard). unknown scope name aborts --mean hint. New summarise_transitions() reduces cohort directed location--location transitions counts mean/median dwell origin state, plot_transition_summary() draws hand-rolled flow diagram (new dependency): nodes ordered along left--right spine, arrowed edges whose width encodes frequency, forward backward transitions bowed opposite ways re-entries stay legible. Every duration statistic respects end_inferred. imputed end non-terminal final stay rendering convenience, data; summariser takes include_inferred = FALSE (default), excluding durations means/medians/quantiles reporting n_inferred_excluded. TRUE folds back , end_inferred flag travelling output. plot_patient_journey(return_data = TRUE) now also returns summary element (summarise_journey_durations() one case), return value list(plot, boxes, events, summary). Additive — existing callers unaffected. New plot_journey_with_summary() (stretch) stacks single case’s timeline per-stage dwell bar chart via patchwork, guarded requireNamespace() install hint.

title: eventviz 0.1.0
text: New plot_stage_ladder() renders strictly linear stage process (complaint, approval pipeline, ticket lifecycle) Gantt-like staircase: stages y-axis first stage top, one horizontal segment per stage, thin grey connectors forming steps, duration labels segment midpoint. reuses derive_location_boxes() verbatim (stages boxes) — rendering differs. plot_stage_ladder() accepts stage_order pin vertical ordering (default: first appearance) stage_targets, named stage -> hours vector rendering light allowance band per targeted stage excess dwell drawn firebrick. terminal stage drawn point marker, segment. plot_patient_journey() gains state_label argument (default "Location") setting fill-legend title, complaint’s boxes read “Stage” ticket’s “Status”. Default output unchanged. New complaint_example dataset: eight complaints moving Acknowledgement -> Triage -> Assigned -> review -> Senior review -> Formal letter sent, patient column (exercising patient_col = NULL). One complaint stalls ~3 weeks “review” one still open, exercising per-stage breach ongoing-spell paths respectively.

title: eventviz 0.1.0
text: New event_log_schema() constructor: classed list bundling time_col/act_type_col/activity_col/case_col/patient_col/ location_categories, print() method. Every field defaults NULL (“part schema”). New autodetect_schema(data, location_categories = NULL) matches column names per-role candidate lists (case-insensitive exact match first, adist() <= 2 fuzzy match), resolving roles fixed order (time -> case -> act_type -> activity -> patient) column claimed one role never reconsidered another. Two equally-good matches one role, unresolved required role, abort naming problem rather guessing. plot_patient_journey() gains schema argument. Precedence per field, highest wins: explicit individual argument (time_col, case_col, …) > matching schema field > function’s existing hardcoded default. Autodetection ever runs schema = "auto" passed literally — passing event_log_schema() object never triggers . Default behaviour (schema argument) unchanged.

title: eventviz 0.1.0
text: New pivot_events_longer() reshapes wide, one-row-per-case milestone data (e.g. arrival_time, triage_time, discharge_time columns) long, one-row-per-event form plot_patient_journey() expects. Handles location vs. non-location milestones (location_cols), custom act_type_map/activity_map overrides, NA-milestone dropping, suffix-stripped auto-labelling ("arrival_time" -> "Arrival"). tidyr moved Suggests Imports (used pivot_events_longer()). Internal: timestamp coercion extracted validate_event_log() shared coerce_datetime_column() helper, reused new pivot function. Fixed pre-existing bug validate_event_log(): filtering single case used bare case_id inside dplyr::filter(), dplyr’s data mask resolve data column named case_id instead function argument whenever case column happened named exactly case_id (natural, common choice — one used throughout new pivot wrapper’s examples). Fixed forcing environment lookup (.env$case_id). change previously-passing behaviour.

title: eventviz 0.1.0
text: Nine defects found executed review, fixed ahead package scaffolding since later stages build corrected semantics. first two intentional default-output changes 0.1.0 (Stage 1f palette): broken behaviour API surface. show_labels = TRUE silently dropped every label (labels nudged hard scale_y_continuous(limits = ...) censored NA). Fixed expanding lower y-range labels actually render. Terminal states (e.g. “Discharged”) extended fake multi-hour stay median tail-inference fallback. New opt-terminal_activities parameter: terminal final move gets zero duration renders vertical marker italic direct label. zero-width-nudge message claimed “stored duration unaffected” duration actually computed nudge. duration now computed nudging, -timestamp stay correctly stores 0. nudged box render hidden behind successor (-timestamp moves). render-xmin_render stagger fixes overlap without touching true xmin event assignment depends . Three cryptic crash paths now abort actionable messages: mistyped tail_strategy, exclude_categories removing every location event, vector/NA case_id. Character timestamps silently parsed UTC regardless data’s actual timezone. New tz parameter threaded lubridate::as_datetime(); POSIXct input keeps tzone. patient_col mandatory. patient_col = NULL now supported event logs secondary identifier. Two side--side legends overflow plot width. Legends now stack vertically. exclude_categories crashed every use actually dropped rows (malformed cli::cli_inform() call message argument).
